mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales indicates strong consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above 5%, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing the cost of capital and dampening corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment and strong housing starts indicates a robust labor market and healthy consumer confidence, which together fuel economic expansion and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both conditions are met, or if the interaction term shows no significant predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth, corporate earnings tend to expand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is amplified when consumer price inflation is contained, preserving real earnings.
failure_condition: If the positive relationship between INDPRO growth and SPX returns disappears or reverses when CPIAUCSL inflation is below 3% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential recession, leading to a decline in corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances following a 0.3pp rise in UNRATE over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which generally supports higher corporate valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over longer horizons.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns show a statistically significant negative correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and corporate margins, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth combined with increasing industrial production signals robust economic activity and corporate expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a medium horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 100K MoM and INDPRO growth > 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where both PAYEMS and INDPRO growth conditions are met.
